title: Question on $\vec{\chi}$-boundedness of $t$-chordal digraphs (Carbonero, Hompe, Moore, Spirkl [3])
statement_text: For which integers $t \geq 3$ is it true that the class of $t$-chordal digraphs (digraphs in which every induced directed cycle has length exactly $t$) with bounded clique number has bounded dichromatic number? That is, for which $t$ is this class $\vec{\chi}$-bounded?
context_text: In their earlier paper [3], the same authors asked for which $t \geq 3$ the class of $t$-chordal digraphs is $\vec{\chi}$-bounded. Aboulker, Bousquet, and de Verclos [1] subsequently answered this negatively for $t = 3$ by constructing 3-chordal digraphs with clique number at most 3 and arbitrarily large dichromatic number. The present paper extends the negative answer to all $t \geq 3$.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every $t \geq 3$ and every $N \in \mathbb{N}$ there exists a $t$-chordal digraph $D$ with $\omega(D) \leq 3$ (and $\omega(D) \leq 2$ when $t > 3$) and $\vec{\chi}(D) \geq N$, thereby answering the question in the negative for all $t \geq 3$.
notes: The question was posed by the identical author group in their prior paper [3]; reference [3] is not fully identified in the extracted text, so attributed_year is null.
paper_title: Digraphs with all induced directed cycles of the same length are not $\vecχ$-bounded
